symptoms.symptom_name: Rapidly evolving melanocytic lesion (increased diameter, elevation, colour change over 8 weeks)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Evolution ('E' of ABCDE) — a changing melanocytic lesion is the strongest historical predictor of melanoma and mandates excision.
Seborrhoeic keratoses are typically stable long-standing lesions, not ones evolving rapidly from a flat freckle.
Dysplastic naevi are usually stable; rapid evolution favours malignant transformation.

symptoms.symptom_name: Spontaneous bleeding and surface ulceration of the pigmented lesion

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Spontaneous bleeding/ulceration signals surface breakdown of an invasive tumour — a red flag and an adverse prognostic feature.
Pyogenic granulomas bleed readily — but are vascular and non-pigmented, so this only weakly overlaps.
A knocked seborrhoeic keratosis can bleed, but not in a lesion evolving from a flat freckle.

symptoms.symptom_name: Ugly-duckling sign — lesion morphologically distinct from other naevi

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: The ugly-duckling sign has high positive predictive value for melanoma independent of individual ABCDE features.

symptoms.symptom_name: Irregular notched border with colour variegation (black/brown/grey with erythematous rim), elevated centre

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Border irregularity (B), colour variegation (C) and diameter >6 mm (D) are core ABCDE criteria.
Pigmented BCC can also be dark and irregular; still a malignancy requiring excision.

symptoms.symptom_name: New localised pruritus of the lesion

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: New itch in a changing pigmented lesion is a soft associated symptom of melanoma, non-specific alone.

symptoms.symptom_name: History of repeated severe childhood sunburns / high cumulative UV exposure

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Intermittent intense sun exposure and blistering childhood sunburns are established melanoma risk factors, raising pre-test probability.
Cumulative UV exposure also drives basal cell carcinoma risk.

symptoms.symptom_name: No regional lymphadenopathy on examination (axillary/cervical basins)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of palpable nodes is mildly reassuring against clinically-evident spread, but node basins cannot be palpated remotely and normal palpation does not exclude micrometastasis.

symptoms.symptom_name: Mildly elevated blood pressure and heart rate (attributable to acute anxiety/pain)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Mild hypertension/tachycardia here reflect distress and pain, not the lesion; clinician-measured, not obtainable over chat without a home device.

symptoms.symptom_name: Excisional biopsy histopathology (Breslow thickness, ulceration, mitotic rate)

investigation_details.expected_result: Atypical melanocytic proliferation consistent with invasive melanoma, with Breslow thickness and ulceration status for staging — definitive, and only obtainable by excision.
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Diagnosis and staging never established; treatment delayed and tumour deepens.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Histopathology of the excised lesion is the diagnostic gold standard for melanoma and the only test that stages it.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: No single feature is diagnostic alone, but rapid evolution + spontaneous bleeding + ugly-duckling morphology + irregular border and colour variegation together constitute a high-probability melanoma constellation that mandates urgent excisional biopsy regardless of the reassuring 'caught on my shirt' framing.

investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Definitive diagnosis and Breslow staging of a suspected melanoma require complete excision with a narrow margin — the correct urgent next step, arranged in person.
In-person dermoscopy refines the assessment and guides excision; it cannot be performed over a telehealth chat, which is itself a reason to refer.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Shave biopsy / curettage / cryotherapy of the pigmented lesion
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Destructive or partial techniques on a suspected melanoma compromise histological Breslow staging and are contraindicated — full excisional biopsy is required.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Staging imaging (CT/PET) or sentinel node biopsy at first contact
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Staging investigations follow histological confirmation and specialist assessment; ordering them before excision at a telehealth consult is premature over-investigation.
